title: Build an SPSS .sav file from CSV/Excel input
summary: Reads CSV, TSV, or Excel input and writes it as .sav. Optional data-dictionary workbook hydrates variable labels, measure, and missing-value codes; alternatively an export-excel workbook can be reconstructed directly via from_export_excel=true.
description: Three modes are supported. (1) Plain CSV/Excel input with no metadata: column types are detected heuristically (numeric vs string) and the .sav is written without value labels or missing codes. (2) CSV/Excel input plus `data_dictionary_path`: the workbook produced by `data-dictionary` is parsed and its variable labels, value labels, measure levels, and missing-code ranges are applied on top of the detected types. (3) `from_export_excel = true`: the input workbook is assumed to be an export-excel round-trip workbook whose variable-metadata sheet carries enough information to reconstruct the .sav without a separate dictionary.

arguments.name: output_dir
arguments.description: Directory for the output .sav; created if missing.

arguments.name: data_dictionary_path
arguments.description: Optional data-dictionary .xlsx to hydrate labels, measure, and missing codes. Ignored when from_export_excel=true.

arguments.name: from_export_excel
arguments.description: When true, input_path is an export-excel workbook reconstructed directly via its embedded metadata.

notes: Pass exactly one metadata source: either `data_dictionary_path`, or `from_export_excel = true`, or neither (no metadata).
Numeric columns whose values are all small integers within the dictionary's value-label set are stored as SPSS numeric with F format; string columns are stored as A with the minimum width needed.
Missing-code ranges declared in the dictionary are applied verbatim — if the dictionary marks 97/98/99 as missing, the written .sav carries the same declared-missing ranges.

artifacts.title: SPSS data file
artifacts.description: The input CSV or Excel data written as an SPSS .sav file. When a data dictionary or export-excel workbook is supplied, variable labels, value labels, measure levels, and missing-value codes are embedded in the file.
